I lived through the 70s, the likes of Red Robbo and his Marxists unions, who destroyed our car industry our bike industry our ship building, the dockers unions destroyed the docks they were meant to protect.. so did the postal unions... the dead weren't buried in Liverpool. , the bins were left to rot for months , delivery of bread was scarce, we had 4 hours of electricity a day, I remember walking with people in the local shop with a candle to help them shop. The miners all walked out holding everyone to ransom, sending flying pickets everywhere and hounding others into striking .attacking them as scabs if they refused , interest rates of near 20% and the wealthy taxed at 80% and above it was dreadful, kier starmer lied his way into power, and burnham will do the same. Labour promised from day one they had fully costed and a plan of action ready, they didn't, unions are again the masters, and will destroy everthing around them.. This is only going to get far worse,

My son was 28 when he got jaundice in May last yeast, no other symptoms It took them until the week before Xmas to finally confirm the pancreatic cancer , as he was so young we were told even after numerous endoscopy’s PET MRI CT bloods it was 99% sure it wasn’t cancer , he was extremely healthy, gym everyday rarely drank/ smoked By then it was too late for the whipple , so first lot of chemo in January failed second lot failed he is now in a hospice and won’t make it his 30th Birthday next weekend . We were told he had up to two weeks and that was 6 weeks ago. He holding on for dear life but there needs to be more done . Not to dismiss this awful cancer just because you are a young fit healthy man

New Age designer Zoe Chan, 40, has been accused of carrying out unauthorised works at the protected former home of Sir Anthony Caro, one of Britain's most celebrated sculptors. The property, part of a leafy Hampstead complex dating back to 1750, was bestowed rare Grade II* listed status because of its historic and architectural significance. However, after buying the residence for £4.1m in 2017, Ms Chan embarked on an ambitious modern makeover turning it into the 'Ground House' - a spiritual retreat with clay walls, timber ceilings, meditation spaces and plunge pool. Guests could even arrange private Brazilian jiu-jitsu classes at the property in Frognal, a highly affluent north London enclave where detached homes have sold for more than £13 million in the past two years.

  • share Architect in row with neighbours over £6m home wellness transformation

'For goodness' sake, pull it up over your chin,' I bark for the third or fourth time since leaving Kyiv. The subject complies, good-naturedly. An olive-green Ukrainian military balaclava I'd bought for the purpose of disguising former British prime minister Boris Johnson is adjusted to my satisfaction. His Mr Punch profile, I'd realised, is almost as distinctive as his famously out-of-control blond thatch. Recognition by strangers, however well-meaning, could put us all at even greater risk on this journey to Ukraine's eastern frontline. To my further, possibly woke, exasperation he now bellows - in those distinctive fruity tones - what has become his balaclava-donning mantra: 'Burka! Burka! Burka!'

I've been on mounjaro for sixteen months and used 10 different suppliers for cost reasons. I've saved a considerable sum as I stored quite a few pens before the Trump price rise in August 25 and made use of the golden dose for the last six pens. I have four months supply in my fridge now as I have found further competitive prices. I expect to be finished after my current stock and will switch to lower dose Mounjaro pills for maintenance. I've lost 8 stone so far and will fight to protect my weight loss. I qualified for an NHS prescription but the waiting list was likely to be a year or more and my health could not wait. It has still been an expensive process but worth it. No regrets. I hope those patients that desperately need help who cannot afford to pay get what is needed. It's a real game changer!

As Wham! took to the stage in Beijing on 7 April 1985, they were making history as the first Western pop act to perform in China.  An audience of 15,000 had come to see the band at the city's Workers' Gymnasium - a rather sterile-sounding venue for two of pop's most glamorous stars - but when George Michael and Andrew Ridgeley ran onstage they were greeted by a cacophony of… silence.  As backing singer Shirlie Holliman says, 'I remember looking at George and I could read his face. There was confusion in his eyes.'

Jennifer Lopez, 56, sizzles in black lingerie before slipping into a white lace one-piece while performing at Dolce & Gabbana's Alta Moda show in Italy

Jennifer Lopez put on a sizzling display as she headlined the closing night of Dolce & Gabbana's Alta Moda show in Taormina, Sicily, on Thursday. The singer, 56, turned heads as she kicked off her electrifying performance in a black lingerie two-piece. She completed her sultry look with matching stockings and suspenders, along with black heels.

Is George Clooney selling his $100M Lake Como party pad? Legendary holiday home has hosted the Obamas, Prince Harry and Meghan Markle and Emily Blunt's wedding

Is George Clooney selling his $100million Lake Como party pad, where he and his wife Amal have hosted the likes of the Obamas, Prince Harry and Meghan Markle? Villa Oleandra mansion is where the film star, 65, first locked eyes with Amal, 48, after she attended one of his lavish dinner parties in 2013. The treasured memory of meeting his wife is what has previously stopped him from selling the property he purchased from the Heinz family in 2002 for $10million.
  • 281 comments

Ysgol Y Garreg village school in Llanfrothen, Gwynedd, north Wales, has become the latest to close its doors because of a declining school roll. Parents have been left 'gutted' by the decision, after previously telling the Daily Mail how their children were receiving 'the best education they'd ever had'. The average cost to educate each pupil was £21,471 - triple the county average of £5,998 - after its student numbers fell by 90 per cent from 17 just one year ago.
  • 1 comment
  • share Britain's smallest school with just two pupils closes after 144 years

Celebrity Gogglebox star Vernon Kay is reduced to tears over young pianist who lost her fingers and legs to meningitis

Celebrity Gogglebox star Vernon Kay was reduced to tears after hearing the story of a young pianist who lost her fingers and legs to meningitis, in the final episode. Grace Matthews, 19, appeared on Claudia Winkleman's Channel 4 show The Piano after her application for the hit show was endorsed by a music therapist at Addenbrooke's Hospital where she was treated. Vernon and his Celebrity Gogglebox co-stars couldn't contain their emotions as they watched Grace's remarkable performance.
  • 28 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Celebrity Gogglebox star Vernon Kay is reduced to tears on the show

well done hope you had a realy nice day out

Claire Howard, from Birmingham, flew to Beauvais, France, for a relaxing day of food, sightseeing and sunshine and was home before midnight. Explaining how she pulled it off, the 48-year-old revealed she spotted return Ryanair flights on Skyscanner for just £27 and booked them a month in advance. 'I've been to Paris a few times and didn't want to travel in from Beauvais as Paris is really pricey and busy,' Claire said.

  • The demented "peace" president who loves war is quickly turning into an expert on football. Soccer actually. .
  • I agree completely. Tuchel believed he'd discovered a "hack" after the Mexico game: go a goal up and then pack the pitch - and box - with defenders. Ironically, England would have had a better chance of winning had we NOT scored early, and instead been chasing a goal in a 0-0 draw near the end. I firmly believe that England is a better side than Argentina. Tuchel turned the tide against his own team. It was a tactical disasterclass.
